sourceMovie.title: Scream VI
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Scream VI works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Scream VI's horror stayed with you, Scream (1996) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The crime through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Scream VI excels in horror, Halloween (1978) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Halloween (2018) is the natural next step after Scream VI: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Halloween II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Scream VI who respond to its horror will find Halloween II (1981) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Scream VI fans keep landing on Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI (1986) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Lady from Shanghai
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lady from Shanghai draws from the same thriller well as Scream VI, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1947 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Changeling
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Changeling (2008) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Scream VI later expanded. If Scream VI resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Scream 4
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of Scream VI — that dark edge, the mystery — translates cleanly into Scream 4 (2011). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Scream (2022) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Scream VI rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Happy Death Day 2U
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Happy Death Day 2U (2019) is the kind of film Scream VI fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Scream VI?
faq.answer: Try Scream, Halloween, Halloween—each shares DNA with Scream VI while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Scream VI?
faq.answer: Scream VI (2023) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Scream VI part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Scream Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
